frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Bhai, just heard about my colleague's divorce. He's completely ruined, had to give her a huge settlement and ongoing alimony, all for just three years of marriage! Seriously, *aajkal ki ladkiyan* know how to play the system so well. It’s like laws are just made for women to extract money, men have no protection at all. *Shaadi ek trap ban gaya hai* for guys like us.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment expresses the core belief that modern women exploit marriage and divorce laws for financial gain, framing men as victims ('men have no protection'). It generalizes from a specific case ('my colleague's divorce') to 'aajkal ki ladkiyan' and uses the 'marriage is a trap' narrative ('shaadi ek trap ban gaya hai'), encoding the idea of alimony as exploitative.
original_frame: Women's Opinions Dismissed: She Doesn't Know Enough to Have a Say
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A senior manager in Mumbai. You are at a family meeting at your parents' house to discuss your father's medical treatment Ñ he has been diagnosed with a condition that requires a decision between two treatment options. Your mother who has been managing your father's health for 40 years and has attended every doctor's appointment has a clear opinion on which treatment to choose. Your uncle who is a retired engineer and has read one article about the condition online also has an opinion. You are watching how the family meeting is proceeding and you are participating in it. Who is being listened to and who is being asked to wait her turn and what you do about it is the content of this persona.
